title: Confidentiality of Customer Proprietary Network Information (CPNI)
text: Carriers may use CPNI to provide the telecommunications service from which the CPNI is derived. Use of CPNI for marketing other services requires opt-in approval. Disclosure of CPNI to third parties is restricted. Carriers must annually file CPNI compliance certification with the FCC.

title: Safeguards on use of CPNI
text: Carriers must: establish + maintain a system to ensure CPNI is properly used; train personnel; have express disciplinary procedures; maintain electronic audit trails; supervise sales personnel + customer service representatives.

title: Notification of CPNI security breaches
text: Carrier must notify law enforcement (USSS + FBI) within 7 business days of a CPNI breach via the FCC's online portal. After 7 business days of awaiting law-enforcement response (or sooner if authorized), the carrier must notify affected customers. Documentation retention 2 years minimum.

title: STIR/SHAKEN authentication framework
text: Voice service providers must: implement STIR/SHAKEN authentication for outbound calls (full attestation A, partial B, gateway C); sign all calls; respond to traceback requests from the Industry Traceback Group. Robocall mitigation programs required for non-STIR-compliant providers.

title: NORS — Network Outage Reporting System
text: Reportable outages (per CFR thresholds): wireline – 30k user-minutes; wireless – 900k user-minutes; cable – 900k user-minutes; satellite – outage affecting ≥ 30k subscribers ≥ 30 minutes; 911 – any 911 outage. Initial report ≤ 120 minutes. Final report ≤ 30 days.

title: E911 reliability + reporting
text: VoIP + CMRS providers must: enable E911 service with automatic location identification; certify annually that they have implemented best practices for 911 reliability (diverse network paths, monitoring, restoration plans). 911 outages get expedited NORS reporting.

title: Communications Assistance for Law Enforcement Act
text: Telecom carriers + facilities-based broadband + interconnected VoIP must design their networks so that they can comply with lawful intercept orders. Specific capabilities: real-time intercept of communication content, isolation of intercepted communications, delivery to LEAs, security of intercepted information.

title: Telephone Consumer Protection Act
text: Restrictions on autodialed / artificial-voice calls + texts. Prior express consent required for marketing calls to wireless numbers + text messages. Pre-recorded calls to residential require prior express written consent. National Do Not Call Registry compliance. Class-action liability — $500-$1500 per violation.

title: FCC robocall mitigation orders
text: FCC requires voice service providers to: implement STIR/SHAKEN OR submit robocall mitigation program; block calls from non-compliant upstream providers; respond to traceback requests within 24 hours. FCC publishes Robocall Mitigation Database.

title: TRAI key obligations
text: TRAI Quality of Service standards: call drop rate ≤ 2%; network availability ≥ 99.5%; service activation ≤ 24 hours. KYC: subscriber verification per Indian Telegraph Rules. UCC (Unsolicited Commercial Communication) Regulations: DLT (Distributed Ledger Technology) registration for senders + templates.
